failure_analysis: Active failure modes: high_reward_low_progress, repeated_event_exploitation, shaping_goal_mismatch. The dominant component is r_contact (event_bonus, weight 20.0) contributing 46.9% of reward magnitude despite triggering only 6.8% of the time. Repeated contact toggling (8 times) indicates the agent is exploiting repeated landing contacts for large positive reward, leading to high reward without actual goal achievement. Dense shaping penalties are always negative and small relative to contact bonus, so agent may focus on triggering contact repeatedly rather than solving task efficiently.
operator_reasoning: convert_to_one_time_event is valid for event_bonus components and directly addresses repeated_event_exploitation by ensuring the agent receives the contact bonus only the first time it makes contact, discouraging repeated bouncing or leg-contact toggling. The target r_contact exists and is of event_bonus type, so operator applies.
diagnosis: Repeated event exploitation from r_contact event_bonus; convert it to one-time reward to remove incentive for multiple contacts.
